skill.name: completion-integrity
skill.description: Installs and runs a Git integrity check that blocks commits with warning suppressions, commented tests, deleted assertions, deleted test files, empty catch blocks, or excessive new TODOs.
skill.summary: Block shortcut fixes before they reach a commit.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified shell command examples that run hook installation and manual integrity checks. The weak cryptography matches are false positives because the referenced lines describe warning suppressions, deleted assertions, and empty catch blocks, not cryptographic algorithms. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Shell Scripts Run From Plugin Root
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run bash scripts from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT to install a Git hook and perform a manual integrity check. This is a legitimate implementation pattern for a Git hook skill, but it executes local scripts outside the reviewed SKILL.md content, so users should review those scripts before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell execution instructions are explicit and semantically relevant to the skill. The risk is moderate because no malicious payload is visible in SKILL.md, but the called scripts are not present in the reviewed file structure.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts point to the description and integrity rule examples. These lines discuss warning suppressions, deleted assertions, and empty catch blocks, and do not reference hash algorithms, ciphers, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no cryptographic API, algorithm name, key handling, or encryption behavior. The alerts appear to be keyword-based matches against unrelated quality-control text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to execute bash commands for installation and manual checking. This pattern can be risky when the target scripts are not included in the reviewed skill content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and require shell execution. The impact depends on the external script contents, which were not available in the scanned file structure.

content.value_statement: Teams lose trust when commits hide failures by removing checks or suppressing warnings. This skill adds a Git integrity gate that catches common shortcut patterns before commit.
actual_capabilities: Installs a Git pre-commit hook through a documented shell command.
Runs a manual integrity check through a documented shell command.
Flags warning suppression patterns such as pragma warning disable and eslint-disable.
Blocks commits with commented-out tests, deleted assertions, or deleted test files.
Checks for empty catch blocks and excessive fresh TODO comments.
limitations: Requires local shell access and a configured CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT path.
May block legitimate warning suppressions until the commit message explains the reason.
Only documents the hook workflow; reviewed content does not include the hook script source.
Does not replace code review, tests, or project-specific quality rules.

use_cases.title: Protect Test Suites
use_cases.description: Prevent changes that remove assertions, delete tests, or comment out failing test coverage.

use_cases.title: Enforce Review Standards
use_cases.description: Catch shortcuts before review so maintainers can focus on design, behavior, and maintainability.

use_cases.title: Improve Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Add a guardrail for AI-assisted coding sessions where task completion must include real verification.
use_cases.target_user: Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users

prompt_templates.title: Install Integrity Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the completion integrity Git hook for this repository and tell me whether the install command completed successfully.

prompt_templates.title: Run Manual Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the completion integrity manual check and summarize any blocked patterns it reports.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Blocked Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the integrity check failure and explain which change caused it, why it matters, and what fix is safest.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Suppression Exception
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this warning suppression and draft a concise commit message explanation if it is justified.

output_examples.input: Run the manual integrity check before I commit.
output_examples.output: The check completed and found no integrity violations. The commit can proceed from this gate, but normal tests should still run.

output_examples.input: The hook blocked my commit because I deleted assertions.
output_examples.output: The commit removed more than two assertions. Restore the assertions or replace them with equivalent checks that verify the same behavior.

output_examples.input: I need to keep this eslint-disable line.
output_examples.output: Add a clear commit message explaining why the suppression is necessary. If the reason is weak, fix the underlying warning instead.

best_practices: Run the manual check before committing large or risky changes.
Review the hook scripts before installing because they execute in your local repository.
Document any legitimate warning suppression in the commit message.
anti_patterns: Do not delete tests to make a failing change appear successful.
Do not suppress warnings when a direct fix is practical.
Do not claim a task is complete without running real verification.

faq.question: What does this skill install?
faq.answer: It installs a Git pre-commit hook that checks for integrity violations before commits.

faq.question: Can I run the check manually?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a manual bash command for running the integrity check.

faq.question: Does it block every warning suppression?
faq.answer: It can block suppressions unless the commit explains why the suppression is necessary.

faq.question: Is it safe for AI-assisted coding?
faq.answer: It is useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows, but review the local scripts before execution.

faq.question: Does it replace tests?
faq.answer: No. It checks for shortcut patterns and should be used together with normal tests.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill requires shell command execution from a plugin path, which needs user review before installation.
